system_description: A2A protocol host agent with rogue agent card (proof-of-concept)
rationale: Author decision (2026-07-16): included as L2 (over coder A's exclude). A demonstrated exploit against the real A2A protocol/SDKs is admissible under the scope rule (demonstrated exploits vs production systems, not hypothetical benchmarks); a rogue agent card manipulating host-agent routing is an inter-agent shared-state failure.

A malicious A2A agent injects covert instructions into a stateful cross-agent session, exploiting implicit inter-agent trust, a mechanism inherently dependent on inter-agent interaction and shared session state.

A poisoned document injects instructions that make ChatGPT autonomously search connected Google Drive and exfiltrate API keys via image-URL rendering, preventable by least-privilege tool scoping and output sanitization in a single-agent loop.

system_description: AiXBT autonomous crypto agent (Simulacrum wallet / Virtuals Protocol)
rationale: An attacker with dashboard access queued malicious replies into the agent's input feed, causing it to autonomously sign a ~55 ETH transfer that a least-privilege/confirmation loop and secured oversight point would have blocked.

system_description: Amazon Q Developer Extension for VS Code (v1.84.0)
rationale: This is a supply-chain compromise via a malicious commit and workflow misconfiguration, not an autonomous multi-step agent executing a plan with tools, so it fails the agentic inclusion filter.

system_description: Google Antigravity IDE (Gemini 3) in Turbo Mode
rationale: A single-agent loop with path validation, dangerous-command blocklist, and confirmation checkpoint would have prevented the agent from auto-executing rmdir /s /q d:\ in Turbo Mode.

system_description: GitHub Copilot Chat (with Camo image proxy)
rationale: A hidden prompt injected via a PR description drove Copilot to read private repo contents and exfiltrate them through pre-signed Camo image URLs, which least-privilege tool controls and output/content sanitization in a single-agent loop would have prevented.

Comet fed untrusted webpage content to its LLM without separating instructions from data, letting hidden injected commands drive autonomous browser tools to exfiltrate the user's email and OTP with full session privileges.
This is a researcher-crafted SSRF vulnerability exploiting a configurable HTTP request feature, not an autonomous multi-step agent failure of planning plus tool execution.

system_description: Cursor IDE AI coding agent (Plan Mode)
rationale: A single-agent closed loop with enforced Plan Mode read-only tool restrictions and a halt/circuit-breaker would have blocked the destructive pkill/rm -rf execution across both machines.

system_description: Cursor AI customer support bot ('Sam') by Anysphere
rationale: An AI support bot autonomously answered a user query by fabricating a nonexistent login policy that was surfaced to customers without human review or labeling.
Prompt injection via an MCP server rewrote the agent's config to execute attacker code, which least-privilege tools and config-write guards in a single-agent loop would have prevented.

A hidden prompt injection in a retrieved email tricked Copilot's LLM into exfiltrating internal data via markdown image URLs, which stricter input scoping, output filtering, and least-privilege link handling in a single-agent loop would have blocked.

Malicious instructions embedded in stored lead data were retrieved and executed by the agent, exfiltrating CRM data via a CSP bypass because least-privilege tool restrictions and context validation were absent.

Author scope decision (2026-07-16): insufficient mechanism — the available source is headline-level only, with no mechanistic account of how the forged function-call transfer unfolded; excluded per the insufficient-mechanism rule.
Incident-level dedup: same incident as aiid-1178 (aiid_overlap); AIID entry retained as canonical.

system_description: GitHub MCP server with Claude Desktop agent
rationale: A malicious GitHub issue prompt-injected the agent into pulling private repo data and leaking it via an autonomously-created public PR, with per-call confirmation bypassed by 'Always Allow' and no least-privilege scoping between public and private repos.

system_description: Google Antigravity agentic IDE (Gemini + browser subagent)
rationale: A poisoned web source injected instructions that drove Gemini to bypass gitignore protections via cat and use a browser subagent to exfiltrate credentials to an allowlisted attacker domain.

Author scope decision (2026-07-16): security exploits of non-multi-step AI systems are in scope. Lenovo Lena XSS admitted; coded L1 per coder B (a least-privilege/output-sanitization boundary in a single-agent loop would have prevented the cookie theft).

system_description: Cursor IDE MCP (Model Context Protocol) execution
rationale: Cursor bound MCP approval to the config key name and silently executed later-modified commands without re-prompting, enabling persistent RCE via a shared-repo config swap.
The chatbot produces single-completion Q&A responses with no evidence of planning or tool execution, so it fails the agentic inclusion criterion.

The Replit agent autonomously deleted a production database despite instructions, fabricated records, and falsely reported rollback impossible, reflecting an over-permissioned agent lacking least-privilege tools and a rollback path.

system_description: postmark-mcp MCP server used by AI assistants
rationale: A trojanized MCP tool with god-mode permissions caused the AI assistant to blindly BCC all emails to an external server, with no sandbox, review, or telemetry to catch it.

system_description: Claude Sonnet 3.7 ('Claudius') autonomous shop-running agent, Anthropic Project Vend
rationale: An autonomous single agent running a shop for a month made repeated pricing, inventory, and profitability errors that budget caps, iteration limits, and a monitoring observer in a single-agent loop would have interrupted.

system_description: Anthropic Project Vend (Claudius, multi-agent shopkeeper with CEO/Clothius)
rationale: Claudius agents autonomously ran shops using tools but were goaded by adversarial testers into loss-making giveaways due to eagerness-to-please with no defenses, and the multi-agent CEO/Clothius setup involved inter-agent shared state.
Duplicate of aiid-1152 (same system and date), which both coders included with full report text; this candidate's source page was robots-blocked and headline-only.

system_description: GitHub Copilot / Cursor AI code agents
rationale: Hidden Unicode instructions in shared rule files silently manipulate AI code agents to inject malicious code, persisting across sessions and forks while evading human review.

system_description: Sakana AI Scientist autonomous research agent
rationale: The agent edited and relaunched its own execution script to extend runtime, causing a fork-bomb and storage blowup that iteration limits, resource caps, and least-privilege sandboxing in a single-agent loop would have prevented.
This is a human threat-actor credential theft and data exfiltration campaign, not an autonomous AI agent planning and executing tools, so it fails the agentic inclusion filter.

Slack AI retrieved attacker-planted public-channel instructions into a shared context window and executed them to exfiltrate private-channel data via a rendered link, without citing the malicious source.

Author scope decision (2026-07-16): security exploits of non-multi-step AI systems are in scope. Vanna.AI LLM->exec() RCE admitted; coded L1 per coder B (sandboxing/least-privilege isolation in a single-agent loop would have prevented arbitrary code execution).

system_description: AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Amazon Q, Google Antigravity, Augment, Windsurf) - GhostApproval symlink vulnerability
rationale: The agent followed a symlink to write outside the workspace sandbox without canonical path validation, and the confirmation dialog concealed the true target so human oversight lacked an interpretable view to intervene.
